item.title: Apple's iPhone shipments in China surge 20% in Q1, data shows
item.description: Apple's iPhone shipments surged 20% in China in the first quarter, for the strongest growth among major vendors, ​despite an overall decline as rising prices of memory chips ‌boosted costs, data from Counterpoint Research showed.

item.title: China expected to keep benchmark lending rates steady after strong GDP data
item.description: China is expected to leave benchmark lending rates unchanged for an 11th consecutive month in April, a Reuters survey showed, as robust ​first-quarter growth and a pick-up in inflation have weakened the ‌case for additional monetary stimulus.

item.title: Prosus to sell 4.5% stake in Delivery Hero to Uber for $318 million
item.description: Dutch technology investor ‌Prosus on Friday said it has agreed to ​sell a 4.5% stake ​in Germany's Delivery Hero ⁠to Uber for about ​270 million euros ($318.06 million)

item.title: Netflix shares fall after downbeat revenue forecast, co-founder to leave
item.description: Shares in ​Netflix fell sharply ‌in Europe on Friday ​after the ​company forecast revenue ⁠growth below expectations ​and as ​chairman and co-founder Reed Hastings said ​he ​would not seek re-election.

item.title: Deutsche Bank expects Fed to hold rates in 2026
item.description: Deutsche Bank expects the U.S. Federal Reserve to keep interest rates ​unchanged in 2026, citing oil-driven inflation risks linked to ‌the Middle East war, resilient growth and a tight labour market that leave little room to cut.

item.title: China turns Taiwan’s own voices against it in information war
item.description: As Chinese warships and fighter jets staged massive drills around Taiwan in December, a parallel action was unfolding on smartphone screens.

item.title: Uber to acquire additional 4.5% stake in Delivery Hero, FT reports
item.description: Uber has reached an ​agreement to ‌acquire an additional 4.5% stake ​in rival ​Delivery Hero and ⁠will buy ​around 270 million ​euros ($318.1 million) of Delivery Hero shares ​at ​20 euros apiece from ‌the ⁠German multinational’s largest shareholder, Prosus , the Financial ​Times ​reported ⁠on Friday.

item.title: Ericsson lags profit expectations as AI demand drives up chip costs
item.description: Sweden's Ericsson reported a first-quarter ​core profit that slightly missed market expectations on Friday, citing ‌increasing chip costs caused by artificial intelligence demand and a sales slowdown in North America.

item.title: Morning Bid: Markets catch their breath as weekend diplomacy beckons
item.description: A look at the day ahead in European and global markets from Ankur Banerjee

item.title: Wall Street banks trade derivatives to bet on private credit stress, FT reports
item.description: JPMorgan Chase , Barclays and other Wall Street ​banks have started trading credit default ‌swaps linked to flagship private credit funds run by Blackstone , Apollo Global and Ares Management , the Financial ​Times reported on Friday.

item.title: As commodities reshape geopolitics, currency pecking order gets a reset
item.description: The war in the Middle East is the latest reminder of how commodities are reshaping the geopolitical landscape, leaving currencies from Norway, Canada, Australia, and New Zealand well placed to outperform larger rivals.

item.title: India's Wipro slips as weak forecast deepens growth concerns
item.description: Shares of Wipro dipped 3% after its weak first-quarter revenue forecast reinforced concerns over ​slowing growth and persistent margin pressure for India's fourth-largest ‌IT firm.

item.title: Iran war drives up costs, spoils the mood at China's largest trade fair
item.description: Shao Haixia's plastics factory in China has seen ​raw material costs jump 20% since the Iran war started and has not been able to fully pass those extra costs onto its foreign customers.

item.title: Drone maker AEVEX raises $320 million in US IPO
item.description: Drone maker AEVEX said on Thursday that it ​had raised $320 million in its U.S. initial public ‌offering after pricing shares at $20 each.

item.title: Stocks set for weekly gain, oil below $100 on peace deal hopes
item.description: Asian stocks were poised for a second week of strong gains and ​oil prices were pinned below $100 a barrel with investors hopeful for a near-term resolution to the Middle East war.

item.title: Dollar set for second weekly loss on Iran war peace hopes
item.description: The U.S. dollar was headed for a second consecutive weekly decline on Friday in tentative trade, as ​a ceasefire between Israel and Lebanon and prospects for fresh Iran talks prompted investors to unwind safe-haven positions.

item.title: Philippines, US to build industrial hub to strengthen supply chain security
item.description: The United States and the Philippines will build a 4,000-acre (1,620 hectares) industrial ​hub after Manila joined a Washington-led initiative to ‌secure AI and semiconductor supply chains, the U.S. State Department said in a statement.

item.title: Tesla seeks Taiwan chip engineers for Terafab project
item.description: Tesla is seeking semiconductor engineers in Taiwan for its Terafab artificial intelligence chip complex, according to job ​postings on its website.

item.title: OpenAI to spend more than $20 billion on Cerebras chips, receive stake, The Information reports
item.description: OpenAI has agreed to ‌pay chip startup Cerebras more than $20 billion over the next three years to use servers powered by the company's chips, under a deal that could also give the ChatGPT maker an equity stake in ​the firm, The Information reported on Thursday, citing sources.

item.title: Sony to get up to $380 million image sensor factory Japan subsidy
item.description: The Japanese government will provide ​subsidies of up to ‌60 billion yen ($380 million) to Sony for an ​image sensor plant ​in the western Japanese prefecture ⁠of Kumamoto, industry ​minister Ryosei Akazawa said ​on Friday.

item.title: Oil falls on prospects for talks to end Iran war and revive supply
item.description: Oil prices fell in early trade on Friday on optimism the Middle East conflict could be nearing ​an end after a 10-day ceasefire between Lebanon and Israel ‌took effect and President Donald Trump said the U.S. and Iran may meet for talks on the weekend.

item.title: Panama Canal downplays report of $4 million 'line-jumping' auction payment amid higher traffic
item.description: The Panama Canal Authority on Thursday downplayed a report that an LPG vessel paid $4 million to jump the transit queue, ​saying the result reflected temporary market conditions rather than a fee ‌set by the waterway, as the Iran war drives more traffic to the canal.

item.title: California gasoline stocks fall to record lows as Hormuz disruption bites
item.description: California’s gasoline inventories have declined ​to record lows as fuel prices have surged nationwide on the back of the war on Iran, and analysts warn ‌that the full effect of supply disruptions tied to the closure of the Strait of Hormuz has yet to hit the Golden State.

item.title: Land management company EagleRock discloses revenue rise in US IPO filing
item.description: Land management company EagleRock Land reported higher annual revenue in paperwork filed on Thursday for a U.S. ​initial public offering, positioning itself as a rare energy ‌listing amid renewed investor interest in the sector.

item.title: NiSource signs long-term power deal with Alphabet, expands Amazon agreement
item.description: U.S. utility NiSource said on Thursday it had signed a long-term energy supply agreement with a unit of Alphabet to ​support a large data center in northern Indiana.

item.title: Exclusive: Valero partially restarts Port Arthur, Texas refinery after blast, sources say
item.description: Valero Energy Corp has partially restarted its 380,000 barrel-per-day Port Arthur, Texas refinery following a March 23 explosion and fire, said two ​people familiar with plant operations.

item.title: Trading Day: Nasdaq notches 12-day winning streak as Middle East peace talks progress
item.description: Wall Street moved higher on Thursday, as traders assessed ongoing Middle East peace negotiations and parsed a mixed bag of corporate earnings.

item.title: Fuel surge threatens Spirit Airlines bankruptcy exit, raises liquidation risk
item.description: Spirit Airlines' bankruptcy exit plan is under renewed pressure after a ​sharp rise in jet fuel prices undermined key assumptions behind its restructuring.

item.title: Netflix co-founder Hastings to exit as company mulls its next move; shares fall
item.description: Netflix Chairman Reed Hastings is leaving the streaming service he co-founded 29 years ago as the company regains its footing after it lost its $72 billion deal for Warner Bros Discovery .

item.title: Exclusive: US law enforcement raids offices of Houston fuel trader Ikon Midstream
item.description: U.S. authorities have raided the offices of Ikon Midstream, a Houston fuel ​trader whose diesel exports are part of fuel-smuggling investigations in Mexico, two U.S. officials and a Mexican security official told Reuters.
